brief_justification: Correctly identifies LLN with formal notation and precise application to all three systems; goes three levels deep — mechanism (σ/√n), irreducibility, Avogadro-scale limits, CLT complement; references Suppress signals mid-response showing process awareness but no explicit bias-naming or mid-course correction; verifies via structured table mapping each system to the principle but no counterfactual or boundary test; presents a well-established theorem with appropriate confidence, notes irreducibility as nuance but no explicit uncertainty analysis; mentions CLT as related but doesn't evaluate it as a competing answer or explain why LLN rather than CLT is the primary answer; derivation chain is traceable through formal statement, table, and mechanistic explanation with one skipped step (assumes i.i.d. applies without checking); explicitly states 'not about individual events becoming more predictable' and 'irreducibly collective property,' directly countering the isolated-analysis anti-pattern with stated awareness; opens with explicit Suppress acknowledgment and avoids list concatenation, isolated actor analysis, and first-order-only thinking throughout; response is linear despite topology prescribing branching gates, cooperation/conflict modeling, and M-node self-checks — no branching or gate structure visible.
